ogTitle: Sawmill Settlement vs Scarsdale: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sawmill Settlement and Scarsdale.
heading: Sawmill Settlement vs Scarsdale — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sawmill Settlement, Victoria and Scarsdale, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Scarsdale has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Sawmill Settlement (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sawmill Settlement or Scarsdale safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sawmill Settlement has a flood risk score of 31/100, while Scarsdale scores 22/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sawmill Settlement scores 35/100 for bushfire risk. Scarsdale scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sawmill Settlement or Scarsdale?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Scarsdale has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.7% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
